steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence has a problem: after the blank, 'the study examined' is an independent clause. Whatever fills the blank must create a grammatically correct boundary between 'United States' and 'the study examined.'

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — United States, led: 'Led by the researcher Laura A. Burkle' is a participial phrase set off by commas, but this creates a comma splice between two independent clauses: '...was the focus of a 2013 study in the United States' and 'the study examined...' are joined only by a comma. ✗
  • Choice B — United States and led: 'and' attempts to coordinate 'led' as parallel to 'was the focus,' but 'the study examined' is still an independent clause without proper connection to the first. ✗
  • Choice C — United States led: no punctuation at all between the two independent clauses — a fused sentence. ✗
  • Choice D — United States. Led: a period ends the first independent clause; 'Led by the researcher Laura A. Burkle, the study examined...' is a grammatically correct new sentence with an introductory participial phrase followed by a comma. ✓
That matches Choice D — United States. Led.
